problem_statement: Trusted identity headers can depend on OIDC/JWKS freshness evidence, but the gateway needs a deterministic receipt assessment that rejects stale, unsigned, insecure, or unbounded JWKS refresh evidence before it can support trusted-header admission.
success_criteria: Fresh HTTPS OIDC discovery and JWKS evidence with issuer pinning, audience binding, bounded algorithms, document hashes, cache freshness, bypass protection, and evidence refs is accepted
Stale JWKS cache evidence is rejected with an explicit blocker code
HTTP discovery or JWKS URLs, missing HTTPS enforcement, and missing redirect blocking are rejected
Unsigned and non-JWKS-bound algorithms are rejected
Invalid discovery or JWKS hashes and invalid key counts are rejected
Non-boolean boundary flags raise explicit errors
The assessment performs no network fetch, no JWT parsing, no request authentication, and no tenant mapping mutation
non_goals: no deployment or production readiness claim
constraints: JWKS freshness must be receipt-backed before it can support trusted-header admission
only JWKS-backed asymmetric OIDC algorithms are permitted
issuer, discovery, and JWKS endpoints must be HTTPS-bound
cache freshness must be bounded by explicit age and TTL fields
errors must be deterministic and auditable
evidence_required: focused OIDC/JWKS refresh evidence test receipt
standalone SDLC requirement and design validation receipt
workspace governance preflight receipt before closure claim
